name: CL.TE basic
description: Content-Length + Transfer-Encoding: chunked. Frontend uses CL, backend uses TE. If responses diverge on the second request, the front/back disagreement is confirmed.

name: TE.CL basic
description: Transfer-Encoding: chunked + Content-Length. Frontend uses TE, backend uses CL. Same disagreement in the opposite direction.

name: TE obfuscation space
description: Transfer-Encoding with trailing space — parsed by one side, not the other.

name: TE obfuscation tab
description: Transfer-Encoding with tab character after header name.

name: Host header injection
description: Replace Host with attacker-controlled value. Watch for password-reset links / absolute redirects that include the injected host.

name: X-Forwarded-Host
description: Many frameworks trust X-Forwarded-Host over Host for URL generation.

name: X-Forwarded-For spoof
description: Spoof originating IP — sometimes used by IP allow-lists for admin panels.

name: X-Original-URL rewrite
description: Some reverse proxies rewrite the request path based on X-Original-URL, bypassing frontend auth.

name: X-Rewrite-URL rewrite
description: Same class as X-Original-URL, different header name (used by some WAFs).

name: Cache poisoning via Host
description: Host header rewritten to attacker-controlled domain; cache may store the poisoned response and serve it to legitimate users.

name: Cache-key confusion
description: Unkeyed query parameter — if backend reflects `utm_source` into the page body but the cache ignores it, a poisoned response can be stored.
